expectations.text: Plan contains all 12 mandatory ExecPlan sections
expectations.evidence: All sections present: Purpose/Big Picture, Progress, Surprises & Discoveries, Decision Log, Outcomes & Retrospective, Context and Orientation, Plan of Work, Concrete Steps, Validation and Acceptance, Idempotence and Recovery, Artifacts and Notes, Interfaces and Dependencies
7 items using '- [ ]' format covering route creation, wiring, tests, lint, manual verification, and build

expectations.text: Plan is self-contained with no external references
expectations.evidence: All knowledge embedded. Defines Express patterns, explains pg.Pool, describes the codebase structure inline. No references to 'see the PRD' or external docs.

expectations.text: Plan uses concrete repository-relative file paths
expectations.evidence: Uses src/routes/health.ts, src/app.ts, src/db/pool.ts, src/__tests__/health.test.ts throughout

expectations.text: Plan includes exact runnable shell commands
expectations.evidence: Includes: npm install, npm run lint, npm test, npm run build, curl -s http://localhost:3000/health, ls src/routes/health.ts

expectations.text: Validation is phrased as observable behavior
expectations.evidence: Acceptance criteria include 'curl http://localhost:3000/health returns HTTP 200 with body {"status":"healthy"...}' and exact test output expectations

expectations.text: Technical terms are defined in plain language
expectations.evidence: Defines: Express.js, Router, pg.Pool, supertest, Jest — each with plain-language explanations and examples of how they manifest in this project
